findings.description: subprocess.run with shell=True executes an f-string built from untrusted job.payload 'source'/'dest', allowing arbitrary shell command injection.
attempt is reset to 0 every iteration before increment, so it is always 1 at the loop guard and run_with_retries loops forever for any job that keeps failing, defeating MAX_RETRIES.
Blocking time.sleep inside async poll_loop stalls the entire event loop; should be await asyncio.sleep.
run_with_retries calls blocking time.sleep and is invoked from async poll_loop, blocking the event loop during every retry backoff.
stop_after is only checked after a job is processed, so poll_loop never terminates when the queue stays empty.
subprocess.run has no timeout, so a hung 'convert' process blocks the worker indefinitely.
Backoff RETRY_BACKOFF_SECONDS * (attempt + 1) is always 2.0 because attempt is reset to 0, so there is no exponential/increasing backoff as intended.
list.pop(0) is O(n) for a FIFO queue; collections.deque would give O(1) pops.
sum(values) assumes numeric payload contents; non-numeric values raise TypeError which, combined with the retry bug, loops forever.
JobQueue uses a plain list with no locking and is not thread-safe if accessed concurrently by poll_loop and drain_sync.
On non-zero returncode the captured stdout/stderr is discarded, so failed conversions give no diagnostic output.
Broad 'except Exception' swallows all error types with only a log line, hiding programming errors.
Return type annotated as bare 'dict' instead of the more specific dict[str, int] used elsewhere in the module.
poll_loop is declared async but never awaits anything, making the async signature misleading.
